name: paddle-billing-blade-mcp
title: Paddle Billing
description: Paddle Billing API operations — products, prices, subscriptions, transactions, webhooks. Token-efficient, write-gated, confirm-gated for destructive ops.
tagline: Subscriptions, products, and revenue through Paddle
author: groupthink-dev

env.name: PADDLE_API_KEY
env.description: Paddle API key from Developer Tools

setup.help_text: Create an API key at Paddle → Developer Tools → Authentication. Choose sandbox for testing or production for live billing.

highlights.text: Products & prices — catalog management with currency support
Subscriptions — lifecycle management, pause, resume, cancel
Webhooks — event delivery configuration and simulation
Sandbox first — defaults to sandbox, production is opt-in
Confirm-gated — destructive operations require explicit confirmation

readme:
### What it does

Paddle Billing Blade MCP gives your AI agent access to your Paddle billing platform — products, prices, subscriptions, transactions, customers, and webhooks. Your agent can check subscription health, look up transaction history, manage product catalog entries, and configure webhook endpoints.

Ideal for SaaS billing operations where you want to query revenue data, investigate customer issues, or manage your product catalog without switching to the Paddle dashboard.

### Sandbox-first

The server defaults to Paddle's sandbox environment (`PADDLE_ENVIRONMENT=sandbox`). This means you can safely explore the full API surface — creating test products, simulating transactions, configuring webhooks — without any risk to production billing data. Switch to `production` only when you are ready for live operations.

### Token efficiency

Paddle API responses include extensive metadata (event history, relationship expansions, pagination cursors). Each tool's concise formatter extracts the fields that matter for decision-making — product name, price, status, customer email, subscription dates — keeping context windows clean.

### Safety model

Billing operations carry real financial consequences, so the safety model is conservative:

- **Reads** work immediately — list products, view subscriptions, check transactions
- **Writes** require confirmation — create products, update prices, configure webhooks
- **Destructive operations** are double-gated — cancel subscriptions, process refunds

The sandbox default adds an additional layer of safety for exploratory use.

scenarios.label: Review subscription health
scenarios.body: List active subscriptions, check for past-due payments or failed renewals, review transaction history for recent charges, and flag accounts that need attention.

scenarios.label: Set up a new product and pricing
scenarios.body: Create a product in the catalog, configure pricing tiers with currency options, verify the product appears correctly, and optionally set up a webhook endpoint for purchase events.

scenarios.label: Investigate a customer billing issue
scenarios.body: Search for the customer's subscription, review their transaction history and payment methods, check for failed charges, and determine whether a refund or subscription adjustment is needed.
